question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a Juju charm can observe, grouped by category:

---

## ⚙️ Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Fired once when the unit is first installed |
| `start` | Fired after initial setup is complete |
| `config-changed` | Fired when charm configuration changes |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fired after the charm code itself is updated |
| `update-status` | Fired periodically (default every 5 minutes) for health checks |
| `stop` | Fired before a unit is destroyed |
| `remove` | Fired for final cleanup before the unit is removed |

---

## 👑 Leadership Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | Fired on the unit that becomes the new leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Fired on all **non-leader** units when leader data changes |

---

## 🔗 Relation Hooks
These are prefixed with the relation endpoint name (e.g. `database-relation-joined`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | When two applications are related |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | When a remote unit joins the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | When a related unit's data changes |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | When a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | When the relation itself is removed |

---

## 🗄️ Storage Hooks
Prefixed with the storage name (e.g. `data-storage-attached`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | Fired when a storage volume becomes available |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Fired just before a storage volume is removed |

---

## 🔐 Secret Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | Fired on secret consumers when a new revision is available |
| `secret-rotate` | Fired on the secret owner when the secret should be rotated |
| `secret-expired` | Fired on the secret owner when a secret reaches its expiry |
| `secret-remove` | Fired on the secret owner when an old revision can be safely deleted |

---

## 🐋 Workload / Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes charms)
Prefixed with the container name (e.g. `myapp-pebble-ready`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | Fired when a container's Pebble daemon is ready |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | Fired when a Pebble health check fails |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | Fired when a previously failed health check passes again |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | Fired when Pebble emits a custom notice |

---

## 📦 Upgrade Series Hooks (machine charms)
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before an OS series upgrade begins |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after an OS series upgrade completes |

---

That's the complete set. Note that relation, storage, and Pebble hooks are **dynamic** — the prefix is determined by the names you define in your `charmcraft.yaml` (endpoints, storage names, container names).
